pattern: Only a value present on every domino can fill an entire row, so testing the two faces of the first domino as the target covers every possibility and rotation counts follow directly.
hints: If some value can fill a whole row, it must appear on the very first domino, on its top or its bottom.
There are only two candidate target values to try: the top and the bottom of the first domino.

For a candidate value, every domino must show it on at least one face; count how many need their top rotated and how many need their bottom rotated, and the answer is the smaller of those two over the feasible candidates.

edge_inputs.description: rotate to make tops all equal
no value appears on every domino
both faces shared so pick the cheaper rotation
value present everywhere but on mixed faces

stress.description: construct a row where a fixed target value appears on every domino, sometimes corrupted
stress.note: mostly feasible rows plus occasional infeasible corruption
